mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate robust consumer spending and construction activity, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive forward market returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a statistically significant positive alpha (t-stat < 1.96) over a 5-year out-of-sample period, or interaction term coefficient is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, rising industrial production demonstrates economic resilience against higher borrowing costs, reassuring investors and triggering short-term positive equity market rallies.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of tightening periods with rising industrial production, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend correlates with expanding corporate profit margins and higher dividend payouts, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over consecutive 3-year rolling windows, or 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with GDP growth deviations from trend.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, elevated inflation erodes real consumer purchasing power and forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 65 percent of high-inflation observations, or the negative correlation between CPI levels and equity returns becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate indicates a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, leading to positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: The interaction model fails to outperform single-series models in out-of-sample testing, or 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 58 percent of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a late-cycle expansion signal weakening economic momentum and reduced construction sector activity, which anticipates lower corporate earnings and drives negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of late-cycle periods with falling housing starts, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50 percent in walk-forward validation.
